title: Chrysanthemum Tea

seo.title: Authentic Chrysanthemum Tea Recipe: Easy Chinese Brew
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Chinese Chrysanthemum Tea at home with our easy step-by-step recipe. Discover the refreshing taste and health benefits of this traditional brew.
h2Headings: How to Make Chrysanthemum Tea: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Perfect Chrysanthemum Tea
seo.imageAlt: A steaming cup of authentic Chinese Chrysanthemum Tea with dried chrysanthemum flowers beside it.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a soothing cup of homemade Chrysanthemum Tea, a traditional Chinese beverage known for its delicate flavor and health benefits.

description: Chrysanthemum Tea is a delicate and fragrant herbal infusion cherished in Chinese culture for its refreshing taste and cooling properties. Made from dried chrysanthemum flowers, this golden brew offers a subtly sweet flavor with floral notes. It is often enjoyed hot or cold, providing a soothing and healthful experience.

ingredients.notes: preferably Hangzhou white chrysanthemums or Huangshan Gongju
filtered or distilled for best taste

instructions.title: Rinse Chrysanthemum Flowers
instructions.text: Place the dried chrysanthemum flowers in a fine-mesh strainer and rinse them gently under cool running water for about 30 seconds. This helps remove any dust or impurities and prepares them for brewing.

instructions.title: Boil Water
instructions.text: Bring 1 liter of filtered water to a rolling boil in a kettle or saucepan. Ensure the water is hot enough, ideally around 100 degrees Celsius (212 degrees Fahrenheit), to properly extract the flavors.

instructions.title: Prepare Teapot
instructions.text: Transfer the rinsed chrysanthemum flowers into a heatproof teapot or a large glass pitcher that can withstand boiling water. Make sure the container is clean.

instructions.title: First Steep (Optional Rinse)
instructions.text: For a purer taste, pour a small amount of the boiling water over the flowers, swirl gently for a few seconds, and immediately discard this water. This quick rinse further cleans the flowers and awakens their aroma.

instructions.title: Main Steep
instructions.text: Pour the remaining 1 liter of boiling water over the chrysanthemum flowers in the teapot or pitcher. Ensure all flowers are submerged. Cover the teapot or pitcher with a lid to retain heat and aroma.

instructions.title: Steep the Tea
instructions.text: Allow the tea to steep for 5 to 10 minutes, or longer for a stronger flavor profile. Observe as the water gradually transforms into a beautiful golden yellow color, indicating the flavors are infusing.

instructions.title: Sweeten and Serve
instructions.text: If desired, add rock sugar or honey to taste while the tea is still hot, stirring gently until completely dissolved. Strain the tea into individual cups, using a fine mesh strainer if necessary, to ensure no loose flowers are present.

instructions.title: Enjoy or Chill
instructions.text: Serve the Chrysanthemum Tea hot for a warming beverage, or let it cool down to room temperature before chilling it in the refrigerator for a refreshing cold drink. The flowers can often be re-steeped 1-2 more times by adding fresh boiling water.

tips: Always use high-quality dried chrysanthemum flowers for the best flavor and aroma. Look for flowers that are whole and have a vibrant yellow color.
Adjust the steeping time according to your preference. A longer steep will yield a stronger, slightly more bitter tea, while a shorter steep gives a lighter, more delicate flavor.
For a refreshing twist, add a few goji berries or a slice of lemon to your Chrysanthemum Tea after steeping, or serve it over ice with a sprig of mint.
article: Chrysanthemum Tea is a beloved Chinese Beverages dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
